76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0

69	class RequestTimingMetricsMixin:
70	    """
71	    Mixin class containing common request-level timing metrics.
72	
73	    This class consolidates the timing metrics that are shared across all batch output types
74	    to avoid code duplication and ensure consistency.
75	    """
76	
77	    # Queue duration: time spent waiting in queue before request is scheduled.
78	    queue_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
79	
80	    # Forward entry time: timestamp when the request enters the forward pass stage.
81	    # This corresponds to `forward_entry_time` in TimeStats.
82	    # In different modes:
83	    #   - Unified/PD-colocate: timestamp when forward computation begins (covers prefill + decode)
84	    #   - Prefill instance (P): timestamp when prefill forward pass begins
85	    #   - Decode instance (D): timestamp when decode forward pass begins
86	    # Note: This is NOT the same as prefill_start_time. There may be a delay between
87	    # forward_entry_time and prefill_start_time (see prefill_launch_delay).
88	    forward_entry_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
89	
90	    # Prefill launch delay: time spent waiting between forward entry and prefill start.
91	    # Calculated as: prefill_start_time - forward_entry_time
92	    # This represents the delay between when the request enters the forward stage
93	    # and when prefill computation actually begins.
94	    prefill_launch_delay: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
95	
96	    # Prefill launch latency: time spent during prefill kernel launch.
97	    # Calculated as: prefill_end_time_host - prefill_start_time_host
98	    prefill_launch_latency: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
99	
100	    # Prefill finished time: timestamp when prefill phase completes (wall clock time).

144	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
145	        _, data = helper
146	        return data, -1
147	
148	    def jump_and_retokenize(
149	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
150	    ):
151	        k = 0
152	        for i, old_id in enumerate(old_output_ids):
153	            if old_id == new_output_ids[i]:
154	                k = i + 1
155	            else:
156	                break
157	
158	        # rollback to the last token that is the same
159	        if k < len(old_output_ids):
160	            self.matcher.rollback(len(old_output_ids) - k)
161	
162	        for i in range(k, len(new_output_ids)):
163	            assert self.matcher.accept_token(new_output_ids[i])
164	
165	    def __repr__(self):
166	        return f"XGrammarGrammar({self.key_string=}, {self.accepted_tokens=}, {self.current_token=})"
167

169	class TokenizerNotSupportedError(Exception):
170	    """Raised when tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar backend."""
171	
172	    pass
173	
174	
175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250:class StreamExecutor:

200	                arguments,
201	                default_sampling_para,
202	                False,
203	                True,
204	            )
205	    else:
206	        pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments)) if progress_bar else None
207	
208	        # Process in chunks to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor
209	        # Otherwise, ThreadPoolExecutor.submit will block after adding certain number of tasks
210	        # so we will never reach "yield" until all tasks are done
211	        chunk_size = 200
212	
213	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
214	            for chunk_start in range(0, len(batch_arguments), chunk_size):
215	                chunk_end = min(chunk_start + chunk_size, len(batch_arguments))
216	                chunk_futures = []
217	
218	                # Submit chunk of tasks
219	                for i in range(chunk_start, chunk_end):
220	                    future = executor.submit(
221	                        run_program,
222	                        program,
223	                        backend,
224	                        (),
225	                        batch_arguments[i],
226	                        default_sampling_para,
227	                        False,
228	                        True,
229	                    )
230	                    if pbar:
231	                        future.add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
232	                    chunk_futures.append(future)
233	
234	                # Yield results from this chunk as they complete
235	                for future in chunk_futures:
236	                    yield future.result()
237	
238	        if pbar:
239	            pbar.close()
240

242	def cache_program(program, backend):
243	    from sglang.lang.tracer import extract_prefix_by_tracing
244	
245	    prefix = extract_prefix_by_tracing(program, backend)
246	    if prefix and len(prefix) > 64:
247	        backend.cache_prefix(prefix)

250	class StreamExecutor:
251	    """A stream executor that executes SGL expressions in a background thread."""
252	
253	    def __init__(
254	        self,
255	        backend,
256	        arguments,
257	        default_sampling_para,
258	        chat_template,
259	        stream,
260	        num_api_spec_tokens=None,
261	        use_thread=True,
262	    ):
263	        from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
264	
265	        self.sid = uuid.uuid4().hex
266	        self.backend: BaseBackend = backend
267	        self.arguments: Dict[str, Any] = arguments
268	        self.default_sampling_para = default_sampling_para
269	        self.stream = stream
270	
271	        self.variables = {}  # Dict[name: str -> value: str]
272	        self.variable_event = {}  # Dict[name: str -> event: threading.Event]
273	        self.meta_info = {}  # Dict[name: str -> info: str]
274	        self.is_finished = False
275	        self.error_ = None
276	
277	        # For completion
278	        self.text_ = ""  # The full text
279	
280	        # For chat
281	        self.messages_ = []  # The messages in the OpenAI API format
282	        self.chat_template = chat_template or self.backend.get_chat_template()
283	        self.cur_role = None
284	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = None
285	
286	        # For vision
287	        self.images_ = []
288	        self.cur_images = []
289	
290	        # For fork/join
291	        self.fork_start_text_pos = None
292	
293	        # For speculative execution
294	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
295	        self.speculated_text = ""
296	
297	        # Worker thread
298	        self.use_thread = use_thread
299	        if self.use_thread:
300	            self.queue = queue.Queue()
301	
302	            def _run_worker_in_context():
303	                self._thread_worker_func()
304	
305	            self.worker = threading.Thread(
306	                target=contextvars.copy_context().run, args=(_run_worker_in_context,)
307	            )
308	            self.worker.start()
309	
310	        # For streaming
311	        if stream:
312	            self.stream_text_event = threading.Event()
313	            self.stream_var_event = {}
314	        else:
315	            self.stream_text_event = None
316	            self.stream_var_event = None
317	
318	    def submit(self, expr: SglExpr):
319	        self._init_var_event(expr)
320	
321	        if self.use_thread:
322	            self.queue.put(expr)
323	        else:
324	            self._execute(expr)
325	
326	    def sync(self):
327	        if self.use_thread:
328	            self.queue.join()
329	
330	    def get_var(self, name):
331	        if name in self.variable_event:
332	            self.variable_event[name].wait()
333	        return self.variables[name]
334	
335	    def set_var(self, name, value):
336	        self.variables[name] = value
337	
338	    def get_meta_info(self, name, timeout=None):
339	        if name in self.variable_event:
340	            got = self.variable_event[name].wait(timeout)
341	            if not got:
342	                raise TimeoutError(f"Timeout while waiting for event '{name}'")
343	        ret = self.meta_info.get(name, None)
344	        return ret
345	
346	    def fork(
347	        self,
348	        size: int = 1,
349	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
350	    ):
351	        if size > 1 and str(self.text_):
352	            self.submit(SglCommitLazy())
353	
354	        self.sync()
355	        size = int(size)
356	
357	        exes = [
358	            StreamExecutor(
359	                self.backend,
360	                self.arguments,
361	                self.default_sampling_para,
362	                self.chat_template,
363	                self.stream,
364	            )
365	            for _ in range(size)
366	        ]
367	        for i in range(size):
368	            exes[i].variables = dict(self.variables)
369	            exes[i].text_ = str(self.text_)
370	            exes[i].messages_ = list(self.messages_)
371	            exes[i].cur_role = self.cur_role
372	            exes[i].cur_role_begin_pos = self.cur_role_begin_pos
373	            exes[i].fork_start_text_pos = len(self.text_)
374	            exes[i].images_ = list(self.images_)
375	
376	            # TODO(ying): handle API speculative execution
377	
378	        return exes
379	
380	    def text(self):
381	        self.sync()
382	        return self.text_
383	
384	    def messages(self):
385	        self.sync()
386	        return self.messages_
387	
388	    def error(self):
389	        self.sync()
390	        return self.error_
391	
392	    def end(self):
393	        if self.use_thread:
394	            if self.worker.is_alive():
395	                self.queue.put(None)
396	        self.backend.end_program(self)
397	
398	    def _thread_worker_func(self):
399	        error = None
400	
401	        while True:
402	            expr = self.queue.get()
403	            if expr is None:
404	                self.queue.task_done()
405	                break
406	
407	            try:
408	                self._execute(expr)
409	            except Exception as e:
410	                warnings.warn(f"Error in stream_executor: {get_exception_traceback()}")
411	                error = e
412	                break
413	            self.queue.task_done()
414	            if self.stream_text_event:
415	                self.stream_text_event.set()
416	
417	        # Clean the queue and events
418	        if error is not None:
419	            try:
420	                while True:
421	                    self.queue.task_done()
422	                    self.queue.get_nowait()
423	            except queue.Empty:
424	                pass
425	            for name in self.variable_event:
426	                self.variable_event[name].set()
427	            if self.stream_var_event:
428	                for name in self.stream_var_event:
429	                    self.stream_var_event[name].set()
430	            self.error_ = error
431	
432	        if self.stream_text_event:
433	            self.stream_text_event.set()
434	
435	        self.is_finished = True
436	
437	    def _execute(self, other):
438	        if isinstance(other, str):
439	            other = SglConstantText(other)
440	
441	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
442	
443	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
444	            self._execute_fill(other.value)
445	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
446	            self._execute_gen(other)
447	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
448	            self._execute_select(other)
449	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
450	            for x in other.expr_list:
451	                self._execute(x)
452	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
453	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
454	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
455	            self._execute_role_end(other)
456	        elif isinstance(other, SglImage):
457	            self._execute_image(other)
458	        elif isinstance(other, SglVideo):
459	            self._execute_video(other)
460	        elif isinstance(other, SglVariable):
461	            self._execute_variable(other)
462	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
463	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
464	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
465	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
466	        elif isinstance(other, SglCommitLazy):
467	            self._execute_commit_lazy_operations(other)
468	        elif isinstance(other, SglConcateAndAppend):
469	            if (
470	                global_config.enable_parallel_encoding
471	                and self.backend.support_concate_and_append
472	            ):
473	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(other)
474	            else:
475	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_text(other)
476	        elif isinstance(other, SglSeparateReasoning):
477	            self._execute_separate_reasoning(other)
478	        else:
479	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type: {type(other)}")
480	
481	    def _execute_fill(self, value: str, prefix=False):
482	        value = str(value)
483	
484	        if (
485	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
486	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
487	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
488	            and not prefix
489	        ):
490	            self.backend.spec_fill(value)
491	            return
492	
493	        if self.speculated_text.startswith(value):
494	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[len(value) :]
495	        else:
496	            self.speculated_text = ""
497	
498	        self.text_ += value
499

257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )

324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer

327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)

335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()

542	                    if stop_pos != -1 and (pos == -1 or stop_pos < pos):

544	                return pos
545	            else:

548	        if stop is None:

551	            comp = self.speculated_text[:max_new_tokens]
552	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[max_new_tokens:]
553	        elif isinstance(stop, (str, list, tuple)):
554	            if self.speculated_text == "":
555	                regen()

557	            if stop_pos == -1:

560	                    len(self.speculated_text),
561	                )
562	            comp = self.speculated_text[:stop_pos]
563	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[stop_pos:]
564	        else:
565	            raise ValueError("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")

567	        return comp, meta_info

569	    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
570	        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
571	        name = expr.name
572	        if not self.stream:
573	            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
574	                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
575	                    self,
576	                    sampling_params=sampling_params,
577	                )
578	
579	            else:
580	                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
581	                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
582	                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
583	                    # They will be lazily executed later.
584	                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
585	                        self,
586	                        sampling_params=sampling_params,
587	                        spec_var_name=name,
588	                    )
589	                    return
590	
591	                else:  # Speculative execution on models with completion interface
592	                    comp, meta_info = self._spec_gen(sampling_params)
593	            if isinstance(comp, list):
594	                self.text_ += comp[0]
595	            else:
596	                assert isinstance(comp, str)
597	                self.text_ += comp
598	
599	            self.variables[name] = comp
600	            self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
601	            self.variable_event[name].set()
602	        else:
603	            assert (
604	                self.num_api_spec_tokens is None
605	            ), "stream is not supported with api speculative execution"
606	            generator = self.backend.generate_stream(
607	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
608	            )
609	
610	            self.variables[name] = ""
611	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()
612	
613	            for comp, meta_info in generator:
614	                self.text_ += comp
615	                self.variables[name] += comp
616	                self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
617	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
618	                self.stream_text_event.set()
619	
620	            self.variable_event[name].set()
621	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()

624	        choices_decision = self.backend.select(

626	        )

629	            self.variables[name] = choices_decision.decision
630	            self.meta_info[name] = choices_decision.meta_info

642	        assert self.cur_role is None, "Nested roles are not allowed."
643	
644	        if len(self.messages_) == 0 and expr.role != "system":
645	            # Insert the default system message

647	            if default_system:

649	                self._execute_fill(default_system)

651	
652	        self.cur_role = expr.role
653	
654	        prefix, _ = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)
655	
656	        self._execute_fill(prefix, prefix=True)
657	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = len(self.text_)

660	        if (
661	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
662	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
663	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
664	        ):
665	            # Execute the stored lazy generation calls
666	            self.backend.role_end_generate(self)
667	        self.cur_role = None
668	
669	        new_text = self.text_[self.cur_role_begin_pos :].lstrip()
670	
671	        _, suffix = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)
672	        self._execute_fill(suffix)

674	        if self.cur_images:

705	    def _execute_concatenate_and_append_text(self, expr: SglConcateAndAppend):
706	        new_text = ""
707	        for s in expr.states:
708	            exe = s.stream_executor
709	            exe.sync()

714	    def _execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(self, expr: SglConcateAndAppend):
715	        self_len = len(self.text_)
716	
717	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
718	            exe = s.stream_executor
719	            exe.submit(SglCommitLazy())
720	
721	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
722	            exe = s.stream_executor
723	            exe.sync()
724	            assert exe.fork_start_text_pos == self_len
725	            self.text_ += exe.text_[exe.fork_start_text_pos :]
726	
727	        src_rids = [state.stream_executor.sid for state in expr.states]
728	        self.backend.concatenate_and_append(src_rids, self.sid)

731	        if self.stream:

735	        if (

750	            cur_text = self.get_var(other.name)

752	            reasoning_name = expr.process_name_for_reasoning(other.name)
753	            self.set_var(other.name, normal_text)
754	            self.set_var(reasoning_name, reasoning)

756	            self.variable_event[reasoning_name].set()
757	            self.text_ = self.text_[: self.cur_role_begin_pos] + normal_text
758	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
759	            for x in other.expr_list:
760	                self._execute_separate_reasoning(

55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
101	
102	    @staticmethod
103	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
104	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
105	
106	    def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
107	        if (
108	            logits.device.type == "cuda"
109	            or logits.device.type == "npu"
110	            or logits.device.type == "xpu"
111	        ):
112	            if _is_hip:
113	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda(logits, vocab_mask)
114	            else:
115	                apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
116	        elif logits.device.type == "cpu" and self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu:
117	            self.apply_vocab_mask_cpu(logits, vocab_mask)
118	        else:
119	            raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported device: {logits.device.type}")

121	    def copy(self):
122	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(

125	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,

135	            ),

55 56 ## About 57 SGLang is a high-performance serving framework for large language models and multimodal models. 58 It is designed to deliver low-latency and high-throughput inference across a wide range of setups, from a single GPU to large distributed clusters. 59 Its core features include: 60 61 - **Fast Runtime**: Provides efficient serving with RadixAttention for prefix caching, a zero-overhead CPU scheduler, prefill-decode disaggregation, speculative decoding, continuous batching, paged attention, tensor/pipeline/expert/data parallelism, structured outputs, chunked prefill, quantization (FP4/FP8/INT4/AWQ/GPTQ), and multi-LoRA batching. 62 - **Broad Model Support**: Supports a wide range of language models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, GPT, Gemma, Mistral, etc.), embedding models (e5-mistral, gte, mcdse), reward models (Skywork), and diffusion models (WAN, Qwen-Image), with easy extensibility for adding new models. Compatible with most Hugging Face models and OpenAI APIs. 63 - **Extensive Hardware Support**: Runs on NVIDIA GPUs (GB200/B300/H100/A100/Spark), AMD GPUs (MI355/MI300), Intel Xeon CPUs, Google TPUs, Ascend NPUs, and more. 64 - **Active Community**: SGLang is open-source and supported by a vibrant community with widespread industry adoption, powering over 400,000 GPUs worldwide. 65 - **RL & Post-Training Backbone**: SGLang is a proven rollout backend across the world, with native RL integrations and adoption by well-known post-training frameworks such as [**AReaL**](https://github.com/inclusionAI/AReaL), [**Miles**](https://github.com/radixark/miles), [**slime**](https://github.com/THUDM/slime), [**Tunix**](https://github.com/google/tunix), [**verl**](https://github.com/volcengine/verl) and more. 66 67 ## Getting Started 68 - [Install SGLang](https://docs.sglang.io/get_started/install.html) 69 - [Quick Start](https://docs.sglang.io/basic_usage/send_request.html) 70 - [Backend Tutorial](https://docs.sglang.io/basic_usage/openai_api_completions.html) 71 - [Frontend Tutorial](https://docs.sglang.io/references/frontend/frontend_tutorial.html) 72 - [Contribution Guide](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html) 73 74 ## Benchmark and Performance 75 Learn more in the release blogs: [v0.2 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-07-25-sglang-llama3/), [v0.3 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-09-04-sglang-v0-3/), [v0.4 blog](https://lmsys.org/blog/2024-12-04-sglang-v0-4/), [Large-scale expert parallelism](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-05-05-large-scale-ep/), [GB200 rack-scale parallelism](https://lmsys.org/blog/2025-09-25-gb200-part-2/). 76 77 ## Adoption and Sponsorship 78 SGLang has been deployed at large scale, generating trillions of tokens in production each day. It is trusted and adopted by a wide range of leading enterprises and institutions, including xAI, AMD, NVIDIA, Intel, LinkedIn, Cursor, Oracle Cloud, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, AWS, Atlas Cloud, Voltage Park, Nebius, DataCrunch, Novita, InnoMatrix, MIT, UCLA, the University of Washington, Stanford, UC Berkeley, Tsinghua University, Jam & Tea Studios, Baseten, and other major technology organizations across North America and Asia. 79 As an open-source LLM inference engine, SGLang has become the de facto industry standard, with deployments running on over 400,000 GPUs worldwide. 80 SGLang is currently hosted under the non-profit open-source organization [LMSYS](https://lmsys.org/about/). 81 82 83 84 ## Contact Us 85 For enterprises interested in adopting or deploying SGLang at scale, including technical consulting, sponsorship opportunities, or partnership inquiries, please contact us at sglang@lmsys.org 86 87 ## Acknowledgment 88 We learned the design and reused code from the following projects: [Guidance](https://github.com/guidance-ai/guidance), [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm), [LightLLM](https://github.com/ModelTC/lightllm), [FlashInfer](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer), [Outlines](https://github.com/outlines-dev/outlines), and [LMQL](https://github.com/eth-sri/lmql). 89

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

115	    def __init__(
116	        self,

118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)

161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)

163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)

177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):

181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

175	class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
176	    def __init__(
177	        self,
178	        tokenizer,
179	        vocab_size: int,
180	        model_eos_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
181	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
182	    ):
183	        super().__init__()
184	
185	        if hasattr(tokenizer, "init_xgrammar"):
186	            # For special tokenizer
187	            tokenizer_info, override_stop_tokens = tokenizer.init_xgrammar()
188	
189	            if tokenizer_info is None:
190	                # Not supported tokenizer
191	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
192	                    f"Tokenizer type {type(tokenizer).__name__} is not supported by XGrammar"
193	                )
194	        else:
195	            # Create TokenizerInfo with model's EOS tokens as the authoritative stop tokens
196	            # This ensures consistency between what the model considers EOS and what XGrammar uses
197	            try:
198	                tokenizer_info = TokenizerInfo.from_huggingface(
199	                    tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, stop_token_ids=model_eos_token_ids
200	                )
201	                override_stop_tokens = None
202	            except Exception as e:
203	                raise TokenizerNotSupportedError(
204	                    f"Failed to create XGrammar TokenizerInfo from tokenizer: {e}"
205	                )
206	
207	        self.grammar_compiler = GrammarCompiler(tokenizer_info=tokenizer_info)
208	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
209	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
210	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
211	
212	    @staticmethod
213	    def _sanitize_structural_format(structural_format):
214	        """Recursively replace missing json_schema fields with an empty schema."""
215	        if not isinstance(structural_format, dict):
216	            return
217	
218	        fmt_type = structural_format.get("type")
219	        if fmt_type in {"json_schema", "qwen_xml_parameter"}:
220	            if structural_format.get("json_schema") is None:
221	                structural_format["json_schema"] = {}
222	
223	        if fmt_type == "tag":
224	            XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(
225	                structural_format.get("content")
226	            )
227	        elif fmt_type in {"sequence", "or"}:
228	            for element in structural_format.get("elements", []):
229	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(element)
230	        elif fmt_type in {"triggered_tags", "tags_with_separator"}:
231	            for tag in structural_format.get("tags", []):
232	                XGrammarGrammarBackend._sanitize_structural_format(tag)
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def _sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag: Dict) -> None:
236	        for structure in structural_tag.get("structures", []):
237	            if structure.get("schema") is None:
238	                structure["schema"] = {}
239	
240	    def _from_context(
241	        self, ctx: CompiledGrammar, key_string: str, grammar_stats: GrammarStats
242	    ) -> XGrammarGrammar:
243	        matcher = GrammarMatcher(
244	            ctx,
245	            max_rollback_tokens=MAX_ROLLBACK_TOKENS,
246	            override_stop_tokens=self.override_stop_tokens,
247	        )
248	        return XGrammarGrammar(
249	            matcher,
250	            self.vocab_size,
251	            ctx,
252	            self.override_stop_tokens,
253	            key_string,
254	            grammar_stats,
255	        )
256	
257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )
318	
319	    def reset(self):
320	        self.grammar_compiler.clear_cache()

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [

168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],

171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report summarizing the SGLang project structure.

## SGLang Project Structure Report

Based on my thorough exploration, here's the complete overview of the SGLang project structure:

### **1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE**

**Main Packages:**
- `/python/sglang/` - Core Python package (the main focus)
- `/sgl-kernel/` - Low-level kernel components
- `/sgl-model-gateway/` - Model gateway services
- `/docs/` - Documentation
- `/examples/` - Example programs
- `/benchmark/` - Benchmarking utilities
- `/test/` - Test suites
- `/docker/`, `/scripts/` - Infrastructure

### **2. SGLANG CORE MODULE STRUCTURE**

**File: `/python/sglang/__init__.py`**

Public API exports:
- **Frontend Language APIs**: `gen()`, `gen_int()`, `gen_string()`, `function()`, `Runtime()`, `Engine()`
- **Role/Chat APIs**: `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()` (and `_begin`/`_end` variants)
- **Sampling Control**: `select()` (for choice selection)
- **Format Control**: `image()`, `video()`, `separate_reasoning()`
- **Backends**: `OpenAI`, `Anthropic`, `LiteLLM`, `VertexAI` (lazy imported)
- **Utilities**: `global_config`, `RuntimeEndpoint`, sampling choices methods

**Main Submodules:**

#### **`/python/sglang/lang/` - Frontend Language Module**

This is the user-facing API layer:

1. **`api.py`** - Public API functions
   - `gen(name, max_tokens, min_tokens, n, stop, stop_token_ids, stop_regex, temperature, top_p, top_k, min_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, ignore_eos, return_logprob, logprob_start_len, top_logprobs_num, return_text_in_logprobs, dtype, choices, choices_method, regex, json_schema)`
   - `function()` - Decorator for defining SGL programs
   - `select()` - Choice selection
   - Role functions: `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()`

2. **`ir.py`** - Intermediate Representation (AST-like structures)
   - **`SglSamplingParams`** dataclass - Contains all sampling parameters including `json_schema` and `regex`
   - **`SglGen`** - Generation IR node
   - **`SglExpr`** - Base expression class
   - **`SglFunction`** - Wraps user functions with `.run()`, `.run_batch()`, `.trace()`, `.cache()` methods
   - **`SglSelect`** - Choice selection IR
   - **`SglRoleBegin/End`** - Chat role markers
   - **`SglImage`**, **`SglVideo`** - Multimodal IR nodes
   - **`SglConstantText`** - Literal text IR
   - Other IR nodes for control flow, variables, etc.

3. **`interpreter.py`** - Execution engine
   - **`StreamExecutor`** class - Main executor that runs SGL programs
     - Executes IR nodes in order
     - Manages variables, messages, text concatenation
     - Handles streaming and batching
   - `run_program()` - Executes a single program
   - `run_program_batch()` - Batch execution with threading
   - `_execute_gen()` - Handles generation calls
   - `_resolve_sampling_params()` - Merges default + override sampling params

4. **`backend/` - Backend Implementations**
   - `openai.py` - OpenAI API backend
   - `runtime_endpoint.py` - **RuntimeEndpoint** - Local/remote SGLang server backend
   - `anthropic.py` - Anthropic API backend
   - `litellm.py` - LiteLLM backend
   - `vertexai.py` - Google VertexAI backend
   - `base_backend.py` - Base backend interface

5. **`chat_template.py`** - Chat templating system
6. **`choices.py`** - Choice sampling methods
7. **`tracer.py`** - Program tracing for optimization

#### **`/python/sglang/srt/` - Runtime (Server) Module**

This is the high-performance serving backend:

- **Frontend (`lang/`)**: User-friendly Python API with IR-based program representation

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report summarizing the SGLang project structure.

## SGLang Project Structure Report

Based on my thorough exploration, here's the complete overview of the SGLang project structure:

### **1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE**

**Main Packages:**
- `/python/sglang/` - Core Python package (the main focus)
- `/sgl-kernel/` - Low-level kernel components
- `/sgl-model-gateway/` - Model gateway services
- `/docs/` - Documentation
- `/examples/` - Example programs
- `/benchmark/` - Benchmarking utilities
- `/test/` - Test suites
- `/docker/`, `/scripts/` - Infrastructure

### **2. SGLANG CORE MODULE STRUCTURE**

**File: `/python/sglang/__init__.py`**

Public API exports:
- **Frontend Language APIs**: `gen()`, `gen_int()`, `gen_string()`, `function()`, `Runtime()`, `Engine()`
- **Role/Chat APIs**: `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()` (and `_begin`/`_end` variants)
- **Sampling Control**: `select()` (for choice selection)
- **Format Control**: `image()`, `video()`, `separate_reasoning()`
- **Backends**: `OpenAI`, `Anthropic`, `LiteLLM`, `VertexAI` (lazy imported)
- **Utilities**: `global_config`, `RuntimeEndpoint`, sampling choices methods

**Main Submodules:**

#### **`/python/sglang/lang/` - Frontend Language Module**

This is the user-facing API layer:

1. **`api.py`** - Public API functions
   - `gen(name, max_tokens, min_tokens, n, stop, stop_token_ids, stop_regex, temperature, top_p, top_k, min_p, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, ignore_eos, return_logprob, logprob_start_len, top_logprobs_num, return_text_in_logprobs, dtype, choices, choices_method, regex, json_schema)`
   - `function()` - Decorator for defining SGL programs
   - `select()` - Choice selection
   - Role functions: `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()`

2. **`ir.py`** - Intermediate Representation (AST-like structures)
   - **`SglSamplingParams`** dataclass - Contains all sampling parameters including `json_schema` and `regex`
   - **`SglGen`** - Generation IR node
   - **`SglExpr`** - Base expression class
   - **`SglFunction`** - Wraps user functions with `.run()`, `.run_batch()`, `.trace()`, `.cache()` methods
   - **`SglSelect`** - Choice selection IR
   - **`SglRoleBegin/End`** - Chat role markers
   - **`SglImage`**, **`SglVideo`** - Multimodal IR nodes
   - **`SglConstantText`** - Literal text IR
   - Other IR nodes for control flow, variables, etc.

3. **`interpreter.py`** - Execution engine
   - **`StreamExecutor`** class - Main executor that runs SGL programs
     - Executes IR nodes in order
     - Manages variables, messages, text concatenation
     - Handles streaming and batching
   - `run_program()` - Executes a single program
   - `run_program_batch()` - Batch execution with threading
   - `_execute_gen()` - Handles generation calls
   - `_resolve_sampling_params()` - Merges default + override sampling params

4. **`backend/` - Backend Implementations**
   - `openai.py` - OpenAI API backend
   - `runtime_endpoint.py` - **RuntimeEndpoint** - Local/remote SGLang server backend
   - `anthropic.py` - Anthropic API backend
   - `litellm.py` - LiteLLM backend
   - `vertexai.py` - Google VertexAI backend
   - `base_backend.py` - Base backend interface

5. **`chat_template.py`** - Chat templating system
6. **`choices.py`** - Choice sampling methods
7. **`tracer.py`** - Program tracing for optimization

#### **`/python/sglang/srt/` - Runtime (Server) Module**

This is the high-performance serving backend:

1. **`sampling/sampling_params.py`** - **`SamplingParams`** dataclass
   - Fields: `max_new_tokens`, `stop_strs`, `stop_token_ids`, `stop_regex_strs`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p`, `frequency_penalty`, `presence_penalty`, `repetition_penalty`, `min_new_tokens`, `regex`, `n`, **`json_schema`**, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`, `ignore_eos`, `skip_special_tokens`, `spaces_between_special_tokens`, `no_stop_trim`, `custom_params`, `stream_interval`, `logit_bias`, `sampling_seed`

2. **`constrained/` - Structured Output/Constrained Generation**
   - **`outlines_backend.py`** - Outlines-based regex/JSON schema constraint backend
     - **`build_regex_from_object()`** - Converts Pydantic models or dicts to regex
     - **`OutlinesGrammar`** - Grammar object for FSM-based decoding
   - `xgrammar_backend.py` - XGrammar backend
   - `llguidance_backend.py` - LLGuidance backend
   - `grammar_manager.py` - Manages grammar compilation

3. **`entrypoints/`** - API servers
   - `engine.py` - **Engine** class - High-level inference API
   - `openai/serving_chat.py`, `openai/serving_completions.py` - OpenAI API compatibility
   - `grpc_server.py` - gRPC server

4. **Other key directories:**
   - `managers/` - Various managers (tokenizer, request, etc.)
   - `models/` - Model implementations
   - `layers/` - Neural network layers
   - `mem_cache/` - Memory caching
   - `function_call/` - Function calling support
   - `multimodal/` - Vision/video support

142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)

650	async def generate_request(obj: GenerateReqInput, request: Request):
651	    """Handle a generate request."""
652	    if obj.stream:
653	
654	        async def stream_results() -> AsyncIterator[bytes]:
655	            try:
656	                async for out in _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
657	                    obj, request
658	                ):
659	                    yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
660	                        out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
661	                    ) + b"\n\n"
662	            except ValueError as e:
663	                out = {"error": {"message": str(e)}}
664	                logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
665	                yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
666	                    out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
667	                ) + b"\n\n"
668	            yield b"data: [DONE]\n\n"
669	
670	        return StreamingResponse(
671	            stream_results(),
672	            media_type="text/event-stream",
673	            background=_global_state.tokenizer_manager.create_abort_task(obj),
674	        )
675	    else:
676	        try:
677	            ret = await _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
678	                obj, request
679	            ).__anext__()
680	            return ret
681	        except ValueError as e:
682	            logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
683	            return _create_error_response(e)
684

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final report:

## Comprehensive Report: SGLang Backend Constraint Processing Flow

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here is the complete flow of how output constraints are processed during generation:

### 1. CONSTRAINT SPECIFICATION (Frontend to Backend)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**Class:** `SamplingParams` (lines 31-243)

Constraints are specified through:
- `json_schema: Optional[str]` - JSON schema for structured output
- `regex: Optional[str]` - Regular expression pattern
- `ebnf: Optional[str]` - EBNF grammar
- `structural_tag: Optional[str]` - Structural tagging format

Constraints are **mutually exclusive** (line 160-161):
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

### 2. API LAYER CONSTRAINT HANDLING

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**Class:** `CompletionRequest` (lines 154-778)

The OpenAI-compatible API accepts constraints via:
- `response_format` parameter with `type: "json_schema"` → maps to `json_schema` in SamplingParams
- Custom `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag` parameters

Constraint conversion happens at lines 750-758:
```python
if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(...)
elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
    sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
    sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(...)
```

### 3. REQUEST PROCESSING IN SCHEDULER

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/managers/scheduler.py`

**Class:** `Scheduler`

At line 1641, the scheduler invokes grammar processing:
```python
added_to_grammar_queue = self.grammar_manager.process_req_with_grammar(req)
```

### 4. GRAMMAR MANAGER - CONSTRAINT QUEUING & COMPILATION

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

**Class:** `GrammarManager` (lines 24-196)

**Key Method:** `process_req_with_grammar()` (lines 67-105)

Process flow:
1. Checks if request has constraints (lines 70-74):
   - `sampling_params.json_schema`
   - `sampling_params.regex`
   - `sampling_params.ebnf`
   - `sampling_params.structural_tag`

2. Creates a cache key: `("json", schema)`, `("regex", pattern)`, `("ebnf", grammar)`, or `("structural_tag", tag_json)`

3. Gets cached or future grammar via `grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value()` (line 89)

4. If cache miss, adds request to `grammar_queue` for asynchronous compilation (line 103)

5. Later retrieves ready grammars via `get_ready_grammar_requests()` which:
   - Polls for completion (lines 121-137)
   - Syncs across distributed ranks (lines 152-163)
   - Returns compiled grammar objects or handles timeouts

### 5. GRAMMAR BACKEND CREATION & DISPATCH

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`

**Function:** `create_grammar_backend()` (lines 206-274)

Selects backend based on `server_args.grammar_backend`:
- **"xgrammar"** (default) - XGrammar backend
- **"outlines"** - Outlines library backend
- **"llguidance"** - LLGuidance backend
- **"none"** - No constraint support

### 6. GRAMMAR COMPILATION - XGrammar Backend

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`

**Class:** `XGrammarGrammarBackend` (lines 175-321)

**Dispatch Methods:**

1. **`dispatch_json()`** (lines 257-270):
   - Calls `grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(schema, any_whitespace)`
   - Handles whitespace pattern configuration via `server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern`

2. **`dispatch_regex()`** (lines 280-286):
   - Calls `grammar_compiler.compile_regex(pattern)`

3. **`dispatch_ebnf()`** (lines 272-278):
   - Calls `grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(ebnf_string)`

4. **`dispatch_structural_tag()`** (lines 288-317):
   - Parses JSON and handles legacy/new formats
   - Creates `StructuralTagItem` objects with schemas
   - Calls `grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag()`

Returns: `XGrammarGrammar` object (a `BaseGrammarObject` wrapper around `GrammarMatcher`)

### 7. GRAMMAR COMPILATION - Outlines Backend

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py`

**Class:** `OutlinesGrammarBackend` (lines 114-179)

**Dispatch Methods:**

1. **`dispatch_json()`** (lines 166-175):
   - Converts JSON schema to regex via `build_regex_from_schema()`
   - Then compiles regex via `_compile_regex()`

2. **`dispatch_regex()`** (lines 177-178):
   - Creates `RegexGuide` from regex pattern
   - Uses `outlines.fsm.guide.RegexGuide`

Returns: `OutlinesGrammar` wrapper with FSM state machine

### 8. CONSTRAINT ENFORCEMENT DURING SAMPLING

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

**Class:** `SamplingBatchInfo` (lines 21-424)

**Key Method:** `update_regex_vocab_mask()` (lines 197-223)

This is called before each token generation:
1. Gets first grammar from batch (line 204)
2. Allocates vocabulary bitmask via `grammar.allocate_vocab_mask()` (lines 207-211)
3. Fills mask for each request via `grammar.fill_vocab_mask()` (lines 217-219)
4. Moves mask to device (line 222)

**Method:** `apply_logits_bias()` (lines 235-248)

Applies constraints to logits:
```python
if self.vocab_mask is not None:
    self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

### 9. CONSTRAINT APPLICATION IN MODEL RUNNER

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/model_executor/model_runner.py`

**Lines 2519-2526**

Before sampling:
```python
sampling_info.update_regex_vocab_mask()
sampling_info.apply_logits_bias(logits_output.next_token_logits)
```

### 10. TOKEN MASKING IMPLEMENTATIONS

**XGrammar Backend** - `apply_vocab_mask()` (lines 106-119 in xgrammar_backend.py):
- Uses `apply_token_bitmask_inplace_cuda()` for CUDA/HIP
- Uses `apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton()` for Triton kernels
- Sets disallowed tokens to `-inf` in logits

**Outlines Backend** - `apply_vocab_mask()` (lines 74-75 in outlines_backend.py):
```python
logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
```

**LLGuidance Backend** - `apply_vocab_mask()` (lines 84-85 in llguidance_backend.py):
```python
apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
```

### 11. TOKEN ACCEPTANCE & GRAMMAR STATE UPDATES

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

**Lines 749-750**

Grammar objects track state in each request:
```python
self.grammar_key: Optional[str] = None
self.grammar: Optional[BaseGrammarObject] = None
```

After token generation, grammar state is updated via `grammar.accept_token(token_id)`

### 12. SCHEDULE BATCH SETUP

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775392166459-96bajm/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

**Lines 2176-2179**

Grammar objects are attached to batch:
```python
if self.has_grammar:
    self.sampling_info.grammars = [req.grammar for req in self.reqs]
else:
    self.sampling_info.grammars = None
```

### COMPLETE FLOW SUMMARY

```
1. User specifies constraint (json_schema/regex/ebnf/structural_tag)
   ↓
2. API layer (OpenAI protocol) converts to SamplingParams
   ↓
3. Scheduler receives request, calls grammar_manager.process_req_with_grammar()
   ↓
4. Grammar manager checks cache, queues for compilation if needed
   ↓
5. Grammar backend (XGrammar/Outlines/LLGuidance) compiles constraint
   ↓
6. Compiled grammar object returned (with FSM/matcher state machine)
   ↓
7. During generation, before sampling:
   - sampling_info.update_regex_vocab_mask()
     → grammar.allocate_vocab_mask()
     → grammar.fill_vocab_mask() (fills allowed tokens per FSM state)
   ↓
8. sampling_info.apply_logits_bias()
   → apply_vocab_mask() (masks disallowed tokens with -inf)
   ↓
9. Softmax + sampling happens on masked logits
   → only valid tokens can be sampled
   ↓
10. Accepted token sent to grammar.accept_token(token_id)
    → updates FSM state for next iteration
```

### KEY CLASSES & FILES SUMMARY

| Component | File Path | Class/Function |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Sampling Parameters | `sampling_params.py` | `SamplingParams` |
| Grammar Manager | `grammar_manager.py` | `GrammarManager` |
| Base Grammar Backend | `base_grammar_backend.py` | `BaseGrammarBackend`, `BaseGrammarObject` |
| XGrammar Backend | `xgrammar_backend.py` | `XGrammarGrammarBackend`, `XGrammarGrammar` |
| Outlines Backend | `outlines_backend.py` | `OutlinesGrammarBackend`, `OutlinesGrammar` |
| LLGuidance Backend | `llguidance_backend.py` | `GuidanceBackend`, `GuidanceGrammar` |
| Sampling Batch Info | `sampling_batch_info.py` | `SamplingBatchInfo` |
| Schedule Batch | `schedule_batch.py` | `Req`, `ScheduleBatch` |
| Model Runner | `model_executor/model_runner.py` | (applies masks during sampling) |
| OpenAI Protocol | `entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` | `CompletionRequest`, `ResponseFormat` |

This comprehensive constraint processing pipeline ensures valid token-by-token constrained generation across all backends with efficient FSM-based filtering.

content.content: 172:class GenerateReqInput(BaseReq, APIServingTimingMixin):

172	class GenerateReqInput(BaseReq, APIServingTimingMixin):
173	    # The input prompt. It can be a single prompt or a batch of prompts.
174	    text: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
175	    # The token ids for text; one can specify either text or input_ids
176	    input_ids: Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None
177	    # The embeddings for input_ids; one can specify either text or input_ids or input_embeds.
178	    input_embeds: Optional[Union[List[List[List[float]]], List[List[float]]]] = None
179	    # The image input. It can be an image instance, file name, URL, or base64 encoded string.
180	    # Can be formatted as:
181	    # - Single image for a single request
182	    # - List of images (one per request in a batch)
183	    # - List of lists of images (multiple images per request)
184	    # See also python/sglang/srt/utils.py:load_image for more details.
185	    image_data: Optional[MultimodalDataInputFormat] = None
186	    # The video input. Like image data, it can be a file name, a url, or base64 encoded string.
187	    video_data: Optional[MultimodalDataInputFormat] = None
188	    # The audio input. Like image data, it can be a file name, a url, or base64 encoded string.
189	    audio_data: Optional[MultimodalDataInputFormat] = None
190	    # The sampling_params. See descriptions below.
191	    sampling_params: Optional[Union[List[Dict], Dict]] = None
192	    # Whether to return logprobs.
193	    return_logprob: Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = None
